text: You offered a good price, and Robert accepted the deal.
Now Robert has questions, and you answer them clearly.
The reason is the new engine.
Good morning, Karina. Why is the price fair?
Let me explain. The reason is the new engine.
I see. Can you give me an example?
For example, you save money on fuel. Certainly, it is faster too.
Good. Can you clarify the discount?
It is five percent this month. Does that answer your question?
For example, you save on fuel.

Good morning, Robert. You have some questions about the deal, and I am happy to answer them. Let me explain the price first. The reason is the new engine. It is faster and safer, so the price is fair. For example, you save money on fuel every month. You asked about the discount too. Let me clarify: the discount is five percent this month. I suppose you want a clear answer, so let me repeat the main points. Does that answer your question?

Hello, this is Robert. Thank you for the proposal. I have a few questions, and I want to be sure I understand. First, can you explain the price again? The reason I ask is my budget. Second, can you clarify the discount? I want an exact number. For instance, is it five or ten percent? Certainly, the aircraft is great. I just want you to repeat the main terms, so everything is clear. Please call me back. Thank you.

First, Robert asked a question about the price. Then Karina said let me explain. Next, she gave the reason and an example. After that, she repeated the main terms to clarify. Finally, she asked does that answer your question, and Robert said yes.
The reason is the new engine.
For instance, you save on fuel.
For example, you save on fuel.
